# UC-22.58.2 · France LPM 24h cyber-incident reporting to ANSSI via CERT-FR — automated submission + receipt evidence

> **Criticality:** Critical &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Compliance, Security, Audit &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*Si un système vital français est touché par une cyberattaque, l'opérateur doit prévenir l'ANSSI sous 72 heures via le CERT-FR. Nous comptons le délai et signalons quand le compte à rebours est en danger.*

---

## Description

Joins ServiceNow LPM-tagged incidents with CERT-FR submission records; computes hours-to-CERT-FR-submission with 72h SLA; surfaces in-progress incidents and breaches.

## Value

Failure to declare to CERT-FR within delay can result in personal sanctions on the Délégué OIV under the Code de la défense.

## Implementation

(1) Configure ServiceNow `lpm_in_scope` flag on SIR records. (2) Build the CERT-FR submission integration (TLS, Webhook, signed payload). (3) Schedule UC-22.58.2 every 30 minutes. (4) Route `breached` severity=critical and Cisco Webex alert to Délégué OIV. (5) Build the `LPM Incident Reporting Health` dashboard.

## Evidence

Saved search `UC-22.58.2` archived every 30 minutes to `audit_evidence` with `lpm_clause="LPM-Art-R1332-41-10"`. Every row pins (incident_id, oiv_id, saiv_id, detection_at, classified_lpm_at, cert_fr_submitted_at, cert_fr_reference, hours_to_submission, submission_status).

## Control test

### Positive scenario

Trigger a test ServiceNow incident with `lpm_in_scope=true` and `priority=critical`. UC-22.58.2 MUST flag CERT-FR submission within 72h.

### Negative scenario

Submit to CERT-FR within window with reference-ID. UC-22.58.2 MUST record `submission_status=on_time`.

## Detailed Implementation

## Prerequisites

- Splunk Enterprise ≥9.2 or Splunk Cloud.
- ServiceNow SIR with `lpm_in_scope` checkbox + `oiv_id` reference field.
- CERT-FR submission integration (TLS, signed JSON payload).

## Step 1 — ServiceNow SIR customisation

Add custom field `u_lpm_in_scope` (boolean), `u_lpm_classified_at` (date/time), `u_oiv_id` (reference). Auto-populate from CMDB SAIV tags.

## Step 2 — CERT-FR integration

Build a Python modular input that POSTs LPM-classified incidents to CERT-FR (over mTLS) and ingests the receipt. Sourcetype `cert-fr:submission`.

## Step 3 — Schedule

```ini
[UC-22.58.2: LPM CERT-FR Reporting]
cron_schedule = */30 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -30m@m
dispatch.latest_time = now
actions = summary_index, notable, webhook
action.summary_index._name = audit_evidence
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.58.2,reg=FR-LPM,clause=LPM-Art-R1332-41-10
alert.severity = 5
```

## Step 4 — Runbooks

**`submission_status=breached` (>72h, no CERT-FR submission)** (owner: Délégué OIV, severity P0):
1. Within 1 hour: escalate to RSSI and CISO.
2. Within 4 hours: submit to CERT-FR with all available evidence + cause-of-delay note.
3. Within 24 hours: document corrective action to RSSI + Direction Générale.
4. Within 7 days: prepare post-incident report for ANSSI inspection if required.

## Step 5 — Troubleshooting

- **LPM-classification trigger**: ensure the classification trigger is conservative (when in doubt, classify in-scope).
- **CERT-FR receipt lag**: receipt may arrive minutes after submission; tolerate small delays.
- **Multi-OIV operator**: classify per OIV.

**FR-specific failure modes**:
- **Personal sanctions on Délégué OIV**: emphasise process discipline.
- **Linguistic precision**: CERT-FR submissions are in French; ensure incident records translated.
- **NIS2 transposition overlap**: 2024 NIS2 transposition introduces parallel reporting; ensure single declaration where possible.

## SPL

```spl
search (sourcetype=snow:incident OR sourcetype=snow:sir) lpm_in_scope=true
| eval detection_at = coalesce(detected_at, opened_at, _time)
| eval classified_lpm_at = coalesce(lpm_classified_at, detection_at)
| join type=left incident_id [search sourcetype=cert-fr:submission earliest=-14d | dedup incident_id | rename submission_id as cert_fr_reference submission_time as cert_fr_submitted_at]
| eval hours_to_submission = case(
    isnotnull(cert_fr_submitted_at), round((cert_fr_submitted_at - classified_lpm_at) / 3600, 2),
    1=1, round((now() - classified_lpm_at) / 3600, 2))
| eval submission_status = case(
    isnotnull(cert_fr_submitted_at) AND hours_to_submission <= 72, "on_time",
    isnotnull(cert_fr_submitted_at) AND hours_to_submission > 72, "late",
    isnull(cert_fr_submitted_at) AND hours_to_submission < 72, "in_progress",
    isnull(cert_fr_submitted_at) AND hours_to_submission >= 72, "breached")
| where submission_status IN ("breached","late","in_progress")
| stats values(oiv_id) as oiv_id values(saiv_id) as saiv_id values(priority) as priority values(short_description) as description max(detection_at) as detection_at max(classified_lpm_at) as classified_lpm_at max(cert_fr_submitted_at) as cert_fr_submitted_at values(cert_fr_reference) as cert_fr_reference max(hours_to_submission) as hours_to_submission values(submission_status) as submission_status by incident_id
| table incident_id oiv_id saiv_id priority description detection_at classified_lpm_at cert_fr_submitted_at cert_fr_reference hours_to_submission submission_status
```

## Visualization

(Panel 1) Open incidents with clock-burn. (Panel 2) Submission status heat-map. (Panel 3) Hours-to-submission distribution. (Panel 4) 12-month submission performance.

## Known False Positives

1. **Pre-classification triage**: a SOC may not yet have classified an event as LPM-in-scope at first detection; the clock starts at LPM-classification.
2. **CERT-FR connectivity outage**: queue gracefully and retry; do not auto-resubmit.
3. **Continuous-update incidents**: long-running LPM incidents may have several CERT-FR updates; track all in `cert_fr_submissions`.

**Suppression mechanism**: KV Store `lpm_22_58_2_exceptions` with Délégué OIV approval and 30-day max.

## References

- [Code de la défense Art. R.1332-41-10](https://www.legifrance.gouv.fr/codes/article_lc/LEGIARTI000028370711/)
- [ANSSI — Déclaration d'incident OIV](https://cyber.gouv.fr/loi-de-programmation-militaire-lpm)
- [CERT-FR portal](https://www.cert.ssi.gouv.fr/)
